name: Sonny Russo
abstract: Santo J. "Sonny" Russo (March 20, 1929 – February 23, 2013) was an American jazz trombonist.

concerts.label: Duke Ellington in His Concert of Sacred Music

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Russo, my roommate on the band... Sonny Russo played — you know we had great musicians on that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Russo--we almost didn't get Sonny. Tommy Mitchell wanted to come as a B flat trombonist... Nick came on the road with us... Joe couldn't--didn't want to, so we had Al Majorca

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Sonny Russo. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah right, Sonny Russo, it was a real good band... They had Don Fagerquist playing trumpet, we had Don Paladino playing lead
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: He was the one that did me with the Italian All-Stars. The sound he got from Sonny Russo and myself… He captured everything crystal clear.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
